edge_hypothesis: Every rsi_pullback spec to date uses SLOW trend EMAs (EMA_200 or EMA_100) with DEEP pullback thresholds (RSI <= 40). This produces very few trades on ETH because: (a) RSI rarely drops below 40 in a trending environment above EMA_200, and (b) EMA_200 is so slow that by the time price is above it, the move is already mature. The result: rsi_pullback has the worst trade count of any accepted template (0-38 trades, fragile). Meanwhile, the dominant market regime is RANGING (per advisory Update 10). In ranging markets, short-term trends form and dissolve within 10-30 bars. EMA_200 is blind to these micro-trends. The untested thesis: use ULTRA-FAST EMAs (EMA_21 trend + EMA_9 slope) to detect micro-trends within ranges. When close > EMA_21, a short-term uptrend is active. When EMA_9 is rising, momentum is accelerating. RSI <= 55 (vs default 40) catches SHALLOW pullbacks — moments where momentum barely pauses before resuming. This is NOT a deep-oversold reversal play (the standard rsi_pullback approach). It's a MOMENTUM RESUMPTION play: price is in a micro-uptrend (close > EMA_21), momentum is accelerating (EMA_9 rising), and RSI has pulled back just slightly from overbought territory (RSI ~45-55 zone). Combined with extreme R:R (7:1+) and time caps, this should produce: (a) MORE trades than standard rsi_pullback because shallow pullbacks are 3-4x more frequent than deep ones, (b) better regime alignment because fast EMAs detect ranging micro-trends that EMA_200 misses entirely, (c) higher PF because the momentum resumption signal is structurally different from the deep-oversold reversal signal — it catches continuation rather than reversal. Invalidation: if PF < 1.0, micro-trends in ranging markets are too noisy for directional entries and the shallow RSI threshold admits too many false signals.

variants.name: rsi_pullback_1h_microtrend_7to1_cap6
variants.description: RSI pullback with ultra-fast EMAs (EMA_21 trend + EMA_9 slope) and shallow pullback (RSI <= 55) on ETH/1h. 7:1 R:R (1.5x ATR stop, 10.5x ATR TP) with 6-bar time cap. This is the core micro-trend test. Standard rsi_pullback uses EMA_200/EMA_50 and produces 0-38 trades. Ultra-fast EMAs should detect short-lived ranging micro-trends and produce 80-150 trades. The shallow RSI threshold (55 vs 40) catches momentum resumptions rather than deep reversals. At 7:1 R:R (proven tier for all 5 ACCEPTs), each winner = 7 losses. Even at 20% win rate, the system is profitable. The 6-bar cap kills trades that don't develop within 6 hours on 1h.
entry_long: Close > EMA_21 (price above ultra-fast 21-period trend — micro-uptrend active).
EMA_9 rising (EMA_9 current > EMA_9 previous — short-term momentum accelerating).
RSI_14 <= 55 (shallow pullback — momentum has paused slightly but not reversed).
entry_short: Close < EMA_21 (price below ultra-fast 21-period trend — micro-downtrend active).
EMA_9 falling (EMA_9 current < EMA_9 previous — short-term momentum decelerating).
RSI_14 >= 45 (shallow bounce — momentum has paused slightly but not reversed).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (10.5x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
Exit at market after 6 bars if neither TP nor SL has been hit (time-decay exit — 6 hours on 1h).
Hypothesis failure mode: if trade count < 50, micro-trend detection is still too selective — try ema_trend=14
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 10.5x ATR_14 from entry.
Time exit after 6 bars at market price.

components.notes: EMA_21 ultra-fast trend + EMA_9 slope detection — micro-trend alignment for ranging markets
RSI_14 <= 55 shallow pullback — momentum resumption, NOT deep-oversold reversal
ATR_14 for 1.5x stop / 10.5x TP — 7:1 R:R with 6-bar time cap

variants.name: rsi_pullback_4h_microtrend_8to1_cap5
variants.description: Same micro-trend concept on ETH/4h. EMA_21 trend + EMA_9 slope + RSI <= 55. At 4h, each bar is 4 hours, so a 5-bar cap = 20 hours (one trading day). 8:1 R:R (1.5x stop, 12.0x TP) gives larger per-trade payoff on the slower timeframe. On 4h, micro-trends last longer — the 21-period EMA covers ~84 hours (3.5 days). This should capture multi-day micro-trends within the ranging macro environment. Comparison: standard rsi_pullback on 4h with EMA_200 has near-zero trades. This ultra-fast variant should produce 40-80 trades on 4h.
entry_long: Close > EMA_21 (price above ultra-fast 21-period trend on 4h — 3.5-day micro-uptrend).
EMA_9 rising (EMA_9 current > EMA_9 previous — 36-hour momentum accelerating).
entry_short: Close < EMA_21 (price below ultra-fast 21-period trend on 4h — micro-downtrend).
EMA_9 falling (EMA_9 current < EMA_9 previous — momentum decelerating).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (12.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
Exit at market after 5 bars if neither TP nor SL has been hit (time-decay exit — 20 hours on 4h).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < 1.0, micro-trends on 4h are too smooth for rsi_pullback to add value vs ema_rsi_atr
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 12.0x ATR_14 from entry.
Time exit after 5 bars at market price.

components.notes: EMA_21 ultra-fast trend on 4h (~3.5-day horizon) + EMA_9 slope for momentum detection
RSI_14 <= 55 shallow pullback — momentum resumption in micro-trends
ATR_14 for 1.5x stop / 12.0x TP — 8:1 R:R with 5-bar time cap (20 hours)

variants.name: rsi_pullback_1h_microtrend_tight_9to1_cap5
variants.description: Tighter variant: EMA_50 trend (slightly longer micro-trend) + EMA_9 slope + RSI <= 50 (stricter — only enter when RSI has genuinely pulled back below neutral). 9:1 R:R (1.5x stop, 13.5x TP) with 5-bar cap. This tests whether the tighter RSI filter (50 vs 55) improves signal quality enough to compensate for fewer trades. RSI <= 50 means momentum has pulled back below the neutral line — a deeper conviction pullback than RSI <= 55. Combined with EMA_50 (10-day moving average on 1h), this detects slightly longer micro-trends. Comparison to V1: if PF_V3 > PF_V1, tighter RSI + longer EMA is the right trade-off for micro-trends.
entry_long: Close > EMA_50 (price above 50-period trend on 1h — 2-day micro-uptrend).
EMA_9 rising (EMA_9 current > EMA_9 previous — 9-hour momentum accelerating).
RSI_14 <= 50 (genuine pullback below neutral — momentum has cooled, setting up for resumption).
entry_short: Close < EMA_50 (price below 50-period trend on 1h — micro-downtrend).
EMA_9 falling (EMA_9 current < EMA_9 previous — momentum decelerating).
RSI_14 >= 50 (bounce above neutral — momentum has recovered, setting up for short resumption).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (13.5x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
Exit at market after 5 bars if neither TP nor SL has been hit (time-decay exit — 5 hours on 1h).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < V1 PF, the tighter filter reduces trade count without improving quality — shallow RSI (55) is the right threshold
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 13.5x ATR_14 from entry.
Time exit after 5 bars at market price.

components.notes: EMA_50 trend (2-day on 1h) + EMA_9 slope — mid-range micro-trend detection
RSI_14 <= 50 genuine pullback below neutral — stricter conviction filter than V1's 55
ATR_14 for 1.5x stop / 13.5x TP — 9:1 R:R with 5-bar time cap
